message:
perf(cache): sampled write maintenance on hot reads to reduce lock contention

Cache reads previously took a write lock on every hit to update frequency
and TTL metadata. Under high concurrency (100+ tool calls/sec), this
serialized all readers through an exclusive lock even when the data was
fresh and valid.

Changes:
- Add read-lock fast path: hot cache hits now peek with a shared read lock
  and only escalate to a write lock every HIT_WRITE_MAINTENANCE_INTERVAL
  (4th) hit for TTL/frequency bookkeeping
- Add S3FifoCache::peek() method for read-only lookups without frequency
  counter side-effects, enabling the read-lock fast path
- Add record_{project,agent}_hit_sampled() metrics helpers that atomically
  count hits and return whether this hit should trigger maintenance
- Update adaptive TTL test to account for sampled hit cadence
- Apply pattern consistently across all cache lookup methods:
  get_project_scoped, get_project_by_human_key_scoped, get_agent_scoped,
  get_agent_by_id_scoped, get_inbox_stats_scoped

The expired-entry write path is preserved as a fallback: if the read-lock
peek finds an expired entry, we fall through to the write lock for eviction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
